display_name: Retatrutide + Tesamorelin/Ipamorelin + MOTS-c IV support logic
first_question: What is the patient actually experiencing: GI intolerance/low intake, poor recovery/low protein, or fatigue/mitochondrial-support request?

ranked_iv_options.why: Best-supported IV support for this stack is symptom-driven hydration because retatrutide’s incretin/glucagon axis has GI adverse-signal overlap and GLP-1 class labels warn about dehydration/renal risk.
Reasonable if reduced intake or B12-deficiency risk exists; not a generic energy or peptide-booster treatment.
ranked_iv_options.evidence_grade: A for B12 risk triggers; C for general IV wellness

ranked_iv_options.why: May support nutrition/recovery context relevant to GH/IGF-1 axis, but no direct evidence it enhances tesamorelin/ipamorelin outcomes.

ranked_iv_options.option: Niagen / NR IV or NAD+ IV
ranked_iv_options.why: This aligns conceptually with MOTS-c mitochondrial axis, but direct MOTS-c + NAD/NR combination evidence is absent. NR/Niagen IV has preliminary tolerability evidence only.
ranked_iv_options.evidence_grade: B/C for NR IV tolerability; D for MOTS-c combination benefit

ranked_iv_options.why: Mechanistic antioxidant support only. No direct evidence it improves retatrutide, GH-secretagogue, or MOTS-c outcomes.
ranked_iv_options.do_not_say: Glutathione detoxes or boosts peptide stack.

output_template: For this peptide stack, IV support is not to enhance the peptides. The most defensible options depend on the patient trigger: hydration/electrolytes for GI/low-intake/dehydration risk; B12/Myers only if intake or deficiency-risk exists; amino/recovery only if low protein/training/recovery phenotype exists; NR/NAD only as provider-reviewed mitochondrial support with limited outcome evidence.
